entities.name: RICE
entities.description: An important food crop (Oryza spp.) essential for food security in the Sahel region of West Africa

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: SAHEL
entities.description: A region in West Africa where rice is mainly grown by smallholders in irrigated systems

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: The geographical area encompassing the Sahel region

entities.name: AFRICA RICE CENTER
entities.description: An organization (AfricaRice) established to increase domestic rice production through research for development

entities.name: RESEARCH FOR DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: A research approach (R4D) aimed at increasing domestic production and improving livelihoods

entities.name: IRRIGATED SYSTEMS
entities.description: Agricultural systems using controlled application of water, where rice productivity is higher in the Sahel

entities.name: POTENTIAL YIELD
entities.description: The maximum yield that can be achieved under optimal conditions

entities.name: ACTUAL YIELD
entities.description: The yield actually obtained by farmers, which is often lower than the potential yield

entities.name: CROP MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The practice of managing crops, including nutrient, weed, and water management

entities.name: NUTRIENT MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The process of optimizing the use of nutrients for crop growth

entities.name: WEED MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The process of controlling weeds to reduce competition with crops

entities.name: WATER MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The process of optimizing water use for crop production

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Non-living environmental factors that negatively affect plant growth, such as extreme temperatures and salt-affected soils

entities.name: EXTREME TEMPERATURES
entities.description: Abnormally high or low temperatures that act as abiotic stresses

entities.name: SALT-AFFECTED SOILS
entities.description: Soils with high salinity levels that act as abiotic stresses

entities.name: CROP SIMULATION MODELS
entities.description: Mathematical models used to simulate crop growth and response to various conditions

entities.name: INTEGRATED CROP MANAGEMENT PRACTICES
entities.description: A holistic approach to crop management to improve productivity and sustainability

entities.name: WATER-SAVING TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Technologies designed to reduce the amount of water used in irrigation

entities.name: AGRONOMIC INTERVENTIONS
entities.description: Specific agricultural practices applied to improve crop performance

entities.name: SALT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant variety to withstand high salinity levels in the soil

entities.name: DECISION-SUPPORT TOOLS
entities.description: Tools that provide data-driven guidance for agricultural management

entities.name: RICEADVICE
entities.description: A specific decision-support tool for field-specific nutrient management in rice

entities.name: SMALLHOLDER LIVELIHOODS
entities.description: The means of securing the basic necessities of life for small-scale farmers

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The capacity of soil to provide essential nutrients for plant growth

entities.name: DOUBLE-CROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two crops on the same piece of land in a single growing season

entities.name: FERTILIZER APPLICATION
entities.description: The act of adding nutrients to the soil to improve crop growth

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Long-term shifts in temperatures and weather patterns

entities.name: HEAT STRESS
entities.description: Stress experienced by plants due to high temperatures, often exacerbated by climate change

entities.name: SOWING DATE
entities.description: The specific date when seeds are planted, which can be adjusted to avoid heat stress

entities.name: VARIETAL IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The process of developing better crop varieties through breeding or biotechnology

entities.name: FARMING SYSTEMS
entities.description: The complex interaction of crops, livestock, and management practices on a farm

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The maintenance of ecological balance and resource health over time

entities.name: SOCIAL SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The ability of a social system to maintain its functions and well-being

entities.name: ECONOMIC SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The ability of an economic activity to be maintained over the long term, often measured by yield and profit

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of crop produced per unit area

entities.name: PROFIT
entities.description: The financial gain made from crop production

entities.name: EXTREME WEATHER CONDITIONS
entities.description: Severe weather events that increase competition for water, land, and labor

entities.name: DIGITAL APPROACHES
entities.description: The use of digital technology to improve agricultural productivity and sustainability
